ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO AMEND

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALICE MOUGHAMIAN'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT, OVER COUNSELS' OBJECTIONS, GRANTS THE FOLLOWING MODIFICATION AS TO BOTH DEMURRING DEFENDANTS: PLAINTIFF HAS 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. MS. TAPIA MAY FILE A THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED WITH THE ADDED MODIFICATION AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MOUGHAMIAN'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CLAIM. THE COURT HAS NOT CONSIDERED PLAINTIFF'S UNTIMELY "SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION," NOR HER DECLARATIONS, WHICH IMPROPERLY RELY UPON MATTERS OUTSIDE THE PLEADINGS. (SEE WORD MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.) OVER THE OBJECTION OF MS. TAPIA, THE COURT GRANTS MS. JOHNSON'S REQUEST TO WAIVE THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Jan-11-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MARGARET S. SCHROEDER, Esq. of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-3843) for defendant City and County of San Francisco;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JANET TAPIA'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTION OF CASE NO. CGC-19-580680 WITH CASE NO. CGC-19-575076.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the sole modification of adding that case no. CGC-19-575076 is designated as the Lead case number, as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to consolidate case CGC-19-580680 with case CGC-19-575076 for trial is granted. Given the number of named defendants in the actions, the court does not see how the demurrer rulings in favor of defendants Moughamian and Houston in case CGC-19-575076 moot this motion.

When ruling on a motion to consolidate the court considers the common issues of law and fact, timeliness (trial date/discovery), complexity of the cases, and prejudice to the parties. Here, those factors weigh in plaintiff's favor. Both actions involve defendants' alleged mistreatment of plaintiff at defendants' treatment centers, there is no trial date in either case, and consolidation is warranted given the overlap. The remaining parties are ordered to coordinate discovery to avoid duplication.

Case number CGC-19-575076 is designated as the Lead case number.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Defendant Felicia Houston'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Janet Tapia, Plaintiff and Self-Represented Litigant, 510.823.5373;
Atty. Sheila Johnson, Esq. of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office, 415.554.4230, for Defendant Alice Moughamian;
Atty. Nicole Meredith, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, 415.398.0200, for Defendant Felicia Houston.

Defendant Felicia Houston's Demurrer To Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court, over counsels' objections, grants the following modification as to both demurring defendants: plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend the complaint. Ms. Tapia may file a third amended complaint.

The tentative ruling is adopted with the added modification as follows:

Defendant Felicia Houston's demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The Court has not considered Plaintiff's untimely "supplemental opposition," nor her declarations, which improperly rely upon matters outside the pleadings.

It is unclear what allegations Defendant Houston is being asked to respond to or what claims are asserted against her. The only allegations referring to her in the lengthy second amended complaint that the Court has been able to locate that have any factual content at all appear on pp. 13 and 26, where plaintiff alleges that on March 6, 2020, Houston, who allegedly managed the Woman's Place clinic and shelter, cancelled plaintiff's reservation. (SAC pars. 40, 77.) In any event, all of plaintiff's claims appear to seek recovery for alleged personal injuries, which are governed by a two-year statute of limitations (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 335.1). As noted, the SAC arises out of plaintiff's discharge on January 5, 2017 from a medical care facility. The original complaint was not filed until April 5, 2019, and plaintiff did not name Houston as a defendant until August 22, 2019, when she filed the first amended complaint. As a result, any claims she may have against Houston are time-barred.

Counsel for Houston is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling with the modification and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
